name: jot
readme:
Short:        Print sequential or random data (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.56

jot - Print sequential or random data (OpenBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  jot [-cnr] [-b word] [-p precision] [-s sep] [-w fmt] [reps [begin
      [end [s]]]]

  Generates sequential or random data: numbers, strings, or characters.

Options:
  -r          generate random values
  -c          character mode
  -n          suppress final newline
  -b word     repeat word instead of numbers
  -p prec     number of decimal places
  -s sep      separator string (default newline)
  -w fmt      printf format string

Examples:
  jot 5            ; print 1 through 5
  jot 5 0          ; print 0 through 4
  jot -r 5 1 10    ; 5 random numbers between 1 and 10
  jot -c 26 a      ; print lowercase alphabet
  jot - 1 10 2     ; print 1 3 5 7 9

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Requires libm (-lm) for math functions. Uses asprintf() from amiport/stdio_ext.h. 41 tests cover sequential output, random mode, character mode, step values, format strings, separator strings, precision, and edge cases with 0 reps and negative ranges.
